headline: Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY) Expands QUILS Lung Cancer Detection Model Beyond Kentucky
summary: Bristol Myers Squibb (NYSE:BMY) Foundation’s QUILS initiative is reshaping lung cancer early detection in Kentucky. The program is now serving as a model for similar efforts in Mississippi and Nevada. QUILS targets earlier identification of lung cancer in regions with some of the highest reported rates in the country. Bristol Myers Squibb operates as a global biopharmaceutical company with a focus on oncology, immunology, and cardiovascular treatments. The QUILS initiative adds a public...

headline: ABBV Leads Its Peer Group. You Pay For That
summary: AbbVie's stock commands a king's ransom, but its performance metrics rule the kingdom, leaving investors to ask if they are buying at the top of the reign.

raw.headline: ABBV Leads Its Peer Group. You Pay For That

headline: Is Janux Therapeutics’ (JANX) Russell Index Exit Quietly Rewriting Its Investor Base and Story?
summary: In late June 2026, Janux Therapeutics, Inc. was removed from several Russell growth and small-cap benchmarks, including the Russell 2000 Growth, 2500 Growth, Small Cap Comp Growth, 3000E Growth, and 3000 Growth indexes. This broad index removal matters because it can force index-tracking funds to rebalance, potentially changing who holds the stock and how it trades. Next, we’ll examine how Janux’s removal from multiple Russell growth indexes shapes the company’s investment narrative for...

headline: SLS Stock Snaps 2-Day Slide: Sellas’ SLS009 AML Trial Spotlights Big Pharma Drug Combo From ABBV, RHHBY And BMY
summary: SLS009 traces back to Sellas’ 2022 GenFleet licensing deal, when the company obtained rights outside Greater China to GFH009, now known as SLS009.

headline: Is Bristol Myers Squibb’s Shift Into Value-Defensive Indices Reshaping The Investment Case For BMY?
summary: In late June 2026, Bristol-Myers Squibb was removed from several Russell growth benchmarks and added to the Russell 1000 Value-Defensive and Russell 1000 Defensive indices, marking a reclassification of the stock within major index families. This shift signals that index providers now view Bristol-Myers Squibb more as a defensive, income-oriented holding than a growth-driven pharmaceutical name, aligning with its dividend profile and efforts to manage patent expirations. Next, we will...
